title: Check tty flag to set default terminal in Env
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->
[podman run -t=false sets the environment variable TERM (docker compatibility) #19264](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/19264#issue-1808085339)

When running podman using the t flag which prevents redirection of standard output.  In Podman, nonetheless of the the flag being provided, TERM was still being set as "xterm".
To prevent this, a flag check for --tty or -t and upon encountering t to be false, the TERM env variable is added in unsetEnv slice which processes it accordingly. If no input is provided for tty flag, then it defaults to false and unsets the TERM from env.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
This PR fixes the Docker compatibility issue where Podman was setting env variable TERM even when a flag -t=false was furnished.
It is fixed now in the following way:
- when running a command: podman run -t=false docker.io/library/alpine printenv TERM, it will print nothing
- when running a command: podman run docker.io/library/alpine printenv TERM, it will default to -t=false
- when running a command: podman run -t=true docker.io/library/alpine printenv TERM, it will print "xterm"
```
Closes #19264

Signed-off-by: Chetan Giradkar <cgiradka@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[diff.patch](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/12131157/diff.patch.txt)
I think something like ^^ would be better.
I would say this PR has a user-facing change. Maybe change the release notes to include a note about how it fixes a docker compat issue and podman no longer sets the TERM env variable?

> [diff.patch](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/12131157/diff.patch.txt) I think something like ^^ would be better.

Thanks @rhatdan , I wasn't sure to change the config template. But yes that's actually better that way.

> I would say this PR has a user-facing change. Maybe change the release notes to include a note about how it fixes a docker compat issue and podman no longer sets the TERM env variable?

Thanks @jakecorrenti to remind me that. I will add it.

LGTM
But did you test 
podman run --tty=false -e TERM=foobar alpine printenv TERM

> podman run --tty=false -e TERM=foobar alpine printenv TERM

I did and it's giving "foobar" as o/p.
LGTM and tests are green. @containers/podman-maintainers PTAL
